generated_code:
# Main entry point - tests cross-module class hierarchy
from colors import Color, RED, GREEN, BLUE, mix_colors, format_color
from shapes import Shape, ShapeType, IShape
from shapes_extended import Rectangle, Circle
from utils import Point, ShapeUtils

def main():
    # Create colors from colors module
    red: Color = RED
    green: Color = GREEN

    # Create points from utils module (struct usage)
    p1: Point = Point(0.0, 0.0)
    p2: Point = Point(5.0, 5.0)

    # Create shapes from shapes_extended module
    rect: Rectangle = Rectangle(10.0, 5.0, red, p1)
    circle: Circle = Circle(3.0, green, p2)

    # Print 1: Shape descriptions showing @override methods
    print(rect.description())
    print(circle.description())

    # Print 2-3: Calculate areas
    rect_area: float = rect.area()
    circle_area: float = circle.area()
    print(rect_area)
    print(circle_area)

    # Print 4-5: Calculate perimeters
    print(rect.perimeter())
    print(circle.perimeter())

    # Print 6: Test ShapeUtils with cross-module types (color blending)
    blended: Color = ShapeUtils.blend_shape_colors(rect, circle)
    print(format_color(blended))

    # Print 7: Test Circle.contains_point method
    test_point: Point = Point(3.0, 4.0)
    print(circle.contains_point(test_point))

    # Print 8: Test midpoint via static factory method
    mid: Point = ShapeUtils.create_midpoint(p1, p2)
    print(mid)

source_files.colors.spy:
# Color system module - provides color struct and utilities
struct Color:
    """RGB color value type"""
    _r: int
    _g: int
    _b: int

    def __init__(self, r: int, g: int, b: int):
        self._r = r
        self._g = g
        self._b = b

    @property
    get r(self) -> int:
        return self._r

    @property
    get g(self) -> int:
        return self._g

    @property
    get b(self) -> int:
        return self._b

    def __str__(self) -> str:
        return f"Color({self._r},{self._g},{self._b})"

RED: Color = Color(255, 0, 0)
GREEN: Color = Color(0, 255, 0)
BLUE: Color = Color(0, 0, 255)

def mix_colors(c1: Color, c2: Color) -> Color:
    """Blend two colors by averaging their components"""
    return Color((c1.r + c2.r) // 2, (c1.g + c2.g) // 2, (c1.b + c2.b) // 2)

def format_color(c: Color) -> str:
    """Format color as RGB(r,g,b) string"""
    return f"RGB({c.r},{c.g},{c.b})"
source_files.shapes.spy:
# Core shapes module - defines base shape types and interfaces
from colors import Color, RED

enum ShapeType:
    """Shape classification enum"""
    GENERIC = 0
    RECTANGLE = 1
    CIRCLE = 2
    TRIANGLE = 3

interface IShape:
    """Cross-module interface for geometric shapes"""
    def area(self) -> float: ...
    def perimeter(self) -> float: ...
    def get_color(self) -> Color: ...

class Shape:
    """Base class for all shapes - NOT abstract so it can be imported across modules"""
    _shape_type: ShapeType
    _color: Color

    def __init__(self, st: ShapeType, c: Color):
        self._shape_type = st
        self._color = c

    def area(self) -> float:
        """Override in subclasses"""
        return 0.0

    def perimeter(self) -> float:
        """Override in subclasses"""
        return 0.0

    def shape_type(self) -> ShapeType:
        return self._shape_type

    def get_color(self) -> Color:
        return self._color

    @virtual
    def description(self) -> str:
        return "Generic shape"
source_files.main.spy:
# Main entry point - tests cross-module class hierarchy
from colors import Color, RED, GREEN, BLUE, mix_colors, format_color
from shapes import Shape, ShapeType, IShape
from shapes_extended import Rectangle, Circle
from utils import Point, ShapeUtils

def main():
    # Create colors from colors module
    red: Color = RED
    green: Color = GREEN

    # Create points from utils module (struct usage)
    p1: Point = Point(0.0, 0.0)
    p2: Point = Point(5.0, 5.0)

    # Create shapes from shapes_extended module
    rect: Rectangle = Rectangle(10.0, 5.0, red, p1)
    circle: Circle = Circle(3.0, green, p2)

    # Print 1: Shape descriptions showing @override methods
    print(rect.description())
    print(circle.description())

    # Print 2-3: Calculate areas
    rect_area: float = rect.area()
    circle_area: float = circle.area()
    print(rect_area)
    print(circle_area)

    # Print 4-5: Calculate perimeters
    print(rect.perimeter())
    print(circle.perimeter())

    # Print 6: Test ShapeUtils with cross-module types (color blending)
    blended: Color = ShapeUtils.blend_shape_colors(rect, circle)
    print(format_color(blended))

    # Print 7: Test Circle.contains_point method
    test_point: Point = Point(3.0, 4.0)
    print(circle.contains_point(test_point))

    # Print 8: Test midpoint via static factory method
    mid: Point = ShapeUtils.create_midpoint(p1, p2)
    print(mid)
